coredata.dc:title: A novel PANI@Carbon dot hybrid with enhanced electrochemical and electrochromic properties
coredata.dc:creator: Li, Xue
Chu, Jia
Cheng, Yaping
Yang, Fan
Xiong, Shanxin
coredata.dc:description: A novel Polyaniline@carbon dot (PANI@C-dot) hybrid was synthesized through copolymerization of aniline in the presence of carbon dots (C-dots) suspension. The PANI@C-dot hybrid thin film exhibits higher optical contrast and faster switching speed compared to the pristine PANI. The enhanced electrochromic performance was presumably due to the synergistic effect between the PANI and C-dots, which improved the electron transport and ionic motion of the electrochromic layer. Thus, the PANI@C-dot hybrid opens a new avenue for developing of C-dots based electrochromic materials and uses in energy conversation and storage devices.
coredata.dcterms:subject: PANI
Carbon dots
Electrochromic
Carbon materials
Hybrid

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (a) XRD patterns and (b) FTIR spectra of the as-synthesized samples.
(a) XPS survey spectra (b) O 1s, (c) N 1s and (d) C 1s spectra of PANI and PANI@C-dot.
(a, b) CV curves and (c, d) UV–vis spectra of the PANI and PANI@C-dot.
(a) Electrochromic response vs. time during potential switching and (b) Electrochemical Impedance spectroscopy of the PANI and PANI@C-dot film.

head.ce:title: A novel PANI@Carbon dot hybrid with enhanced electrochemical and electrochromic properties

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering
Xi’an University of Science and Technology

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: A novel Polyaniline@carbon dot (PANI@C-dot) hybrid was synthesized through copolymerization of aniline in the presence of carbon dots (C-dots) suspension. The PANI@C-dot hybrid thin film exhibits higher optical contrast and faster switching speed compared to the pristine PANI. The enhanced electrochromic performance was presumably due to the synergistic effect between the PANI and C-dots, which improved the electron transport and ionic motion of the electrochromic layer. Thus, the PANI@C-dot hybrid opens a new avenue for developing of C-dots based electrochromic materials and uses in energy conversation and storage devices.

ce:section.ce:para: PANI(Polyaniline), a common conducting polymer, was widely confirmed as an ideal electrochromic material because it has many beneficial properties such as higher color efficiency, lower material cost and greater durability than most other conducting polymers [1]. Despite their benefits, the limitations in optical contrast and cycling life of the PANI electrode associated with poor stability and conductivity is still waiting to be improved to meet the requirements of practical application in electrochromimic devices (ECDs). In this regard, employing carbonaceous materials were demonstrated to be a reliable strategy to address these concerns [2]. Graphene oxide for example offers both high conductivity and surface area. There have been many reports on the development of carbon-based composites for electrochromic applications, such as graphene, grapheme oxide (GO), reduced graphene oxide (RGO) and carbon nanotubes (CNTs). These studies claimed faster electro/ion transfer kinetics due to the synergistic effect between carbonaceous materials and PANI [3–5].

In recent years, C-dots have emerged as promising 0D material owing to their exceptional physicochemical and photochemical stability as well as excellent electrochemical properties [6–8]. Furthermore, C-dots have been exhibited promise in the field of electronic applications due to their highly tailorable surface chemistry, good electrical conductivity and high mobility [9]. These areas include sensors, solar cells and light emitting diodes [10–12]. Recently, C-dots were also considered as new electrode material that can improve the electronic transport, ionic motion and enlarge the contact area between electrode and electrolyte in energy storage devices [13]. Considering the exceptional conducting property and optical transparency of C-dots material, we anticipated that the electrochromic properties of the PANI-based electrode material would be significantly enhanced in presence of C-dots, owing to incorporating with PANI chains or adsorbing through electrostatic interaction or hydrogen bond [14]. However, there are very few studies devoted to the electrochromic applications using C-dots.

In this work, given that C-dots being soluble in aqueous medium, a novel hybrid electrode material of PANI@C-dot was fabricated by directly copolymerization the PANI with C-dots. Further, we explored the PANI@C-dot hybrid for electrochromic uses. The encouraging results make the unique PANI@C-dot hybrid a promising candidate for the electrochromic application with excellent response properties.

All the reagents are of analytical grade and used without further purification. Preparation of C-dots was carried out according to the reported method [15]. The PANI@C-dot hybrid was synthesized as follows: 0.465 g of aniline, 5.11 g of polystyrene sulfonate (APS), 4.65 mg of C-dots and 26 ml of deionized water were mixed and stirred in an ice bath. Afterwards, the polymerization reaction was triggered by slowly dropwise addition of a mixture of 1.14 g ammonium persulphate (PSS) in 10 ml water. The reaction was then allowed to react for 24 h. The final product was denoted as PANI@C-dot hybrid. Meanwhile, parallel experiment with PANI was also carried out, which was prepared using the same procedure as PANI@C-dot without addition of C-dots.

The devices were fabricated by spin coating method as described in our previous work [4]. It was sealed with epoxy resin giving an effective area of about 2.5 × 2.5 cm2. The XRD patterns were carried out using a Rigaku/DMAX 2400 diffractometer with CuKα radiation (λ = 1.54178 Å). The FTIR spectrum was acquired on a Perkin-Elmer Spectrum 100 Series spectrometer. The chemical composition of the samples was characterized by XPS (Thermo Fisher, Massachusetts, USA). The CV (cyclic voltammetry) test and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) were carried out on an Autolab PGSTAT302N workstation in a three-electrode environment with spin-coated film on ITO glass as the working electrode, Pt sheet as the counter electrode and Ag/AgCl as the reference electrode in a 0.1 M LiClO4/PC electrolyte solution. Electrochromic properties of the hybrid were recorded on UV–Vis 2550 spectrophotometer, by applying constant potentials and square-wave potentials using an Autolab electrochemical workstation.

The XRD patterns of C-dots, PANI and PANI@C-dot hybrid are shown in Fig. 1 a. XRD diffractogram show that the C-dots are amorphous carbon network. The peaks centered at 18.5° and 25.6° were identified as the (0 2 0) and (2 0 0) reflection of PANI, consistent with previous reports [16]. It is noticeable, the PANI@C-dot exhibits higher intensity of crystallinity than PANI, and nearly no C-dots peaks are observed, which might be due to the really slight amount of C-dots in the hybrid. Fig. 1b shows the FTIR spectra of the as-synthesized samples. For C-dots, the characteristic bands at 3421, 1659 and 1187 cm−1 are attributed to the stretching vibrations of OH, CO and COC, respectively. In the case of PANI and PANI@C-dot hybrid, the peak observed at 1560 cm−1 shows CC stretching vibration, which indicate the conductivity of the as-synthesized hybrid [5].

In order to further confirm the formation of PANI@C-dot hybrid, XPS spectra were performed for PANI before and after hybridization with C-dots. As shown in Fig. 2 a–d, after hybridization, the peak intensities from oxygen-related and nitrogen-related groups increase significantly. In addition, compared with the spectra of PANI, the spectra of PANI@C-dot shifted to higher energies, which confirming that C-dots have been successfully grafted with the PANI structures.

The electrochemical performance of pure PANI and PANI@C-dot hybrid were evaluated using CV analysis at various scan rates. As shown in Fig. 3 a and b, the enclosed areas in the CV curves of PANI@C-dot are much larger than that of PANI film, indicating that the incorporation of C-dots can enhance the electrochemical performance of PANI and the PANI@C-dot has the better conductivity that can realize fast electron transfer behavior. Based on the structural characterizations and CV properties, we convincingly see the following reasons for enhancing the electrochromic performance of PANI@C-dot hybrid: (i) the introduction of C-dots into PANI bring inner connection between C-dots and PANI; (ii) the C-dots increase the number of electrochemical active sites that in contact with the electrolyte.

The absorption spectra in the wavelength range of 400–850 nm in colored and bleached states of the samples are shown in Fig. 3c and d. The maximum optical contrast of the PANI@C-dot (58.8%) is higher than that of PANI (41.1%), which reflected the incorporation of C-dots into PANI. The kinetic switching response of the PANI and PANI@C-dot hybrid at a wavelength of 650 nm with the potential switches between −2.4 and +2.2 V were measured, as shown in Fig. 4 a. As expected, under a switching step of 20 s, the PANI without C-dots interconnecting exhibits inferior electrochromic response with tc (the coloration time to reach 90% of color change) of 1.9 s. While, PANI@C-dot shows the better performance with tc of 1.0 s. The kinetic curves for PANI@C-dot hybrid and PANI are stable and consistent without significant change upon 5 repetitive coloring/bleaching switching. These improved results for the hybrid film can be simply interpreted by the reinforcement of the electrochemical activity and large surface area of C-dots which facilitates the anion insertion or extraction process within the PANI@C-dot hybrid film. As shown in Fig. 4b, the Nyquist plots of the PANI@C-dot hybrid indicate significantly low ohmic and kinetic impedances compared with PANI, which again proves an easier ion diffusion process for ion insertion and extraction and superior electrical conductivity for PANI@C-dot hybrid. Consequently, the advantages of PANI@C-dot hybrid made C-dots a prominent electrode material for future C-dots based electrochromic uses.

A simple approach to synthesize the PANI@C-dot hybrid is developed through copolymerization of PANI in the presence of C-dots suspension. The as-prepared PANI@C-dot hybrid exhibits superior electrochromic performance with higher optical contrast and faster response time compared to the pristine PANI. The enhancement of electrochromic performance is attributed to the formation of herterostructure, which improves the electron transfer and ion motion behavior. These results demostrate that PANI@C-dot hybrid is a promising candidate for fabrication of efficient electrochromic device, thus provides a platform for the use of C-dots as a constituent material for electrochromic applications.

Xue Li: Writing - original draft, Data curation, Formal analysis. Jia Chu: Funding acquisition, Writing - original draft, Investigation. Yaping Cheng: Methodology, Formal analysis. Fan Yang: Investigation, Supervision. Shanxin Xiong: Writing - review & editing.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (NSFC, No. 51503169) and the project from Xi'an University of Science and Technology (No. 2019YQ2-10).
